fundamentals.name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs and develops the constellation of BlueBird satellites in the United States. The company provides a cellular broadband network in space to be accessible directly by smartphones for commercial use and other applications, as well as for government use. Its SpaceMobile service provides cellular broadband services to end-users who are out of terrestrial cellular coverage. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but with extreme operating losses and a high valuation, balanced by substantial cash reserves.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by a wide margin; next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Revenue growth 1952.2% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Total cash $3.03B — ample liquidity for operations.
Gross margin 44.8% — healthy unit economics on services provided.
negatives: Forward P/E -435.67x — valuation is extremely stretched.
Price/Sales (TTM) 591.54x — trading at a very high multiple of sales.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-1.41B — significant cash burn.
Last quarter EPS surprise -233.7% — substantial earnings miss.

news.paragraph: AST SpaceMobile is experiencing a significant surge, up 35.9% after receiving FCC approval and resetting its BlueBird satellite launch schedule. The company's stock has rallied strongly, adding $10 billion in market cap on bold industry developments, including a spotlight on its Vodafone joint venture amid Europe's push against Starlink. While space stocks are generally soaring, red flags are being raised due to the rapid ascent of companies like ASTS.
